test_description: For each masked temperature map, fit and subtract the monopole and dipole on the unmasked pixels, then compute the masked pseudo-power spectrum \(\tilde C_\ell\) for \(2 \le \ell \le 30\) using the same mask for the Planck map and all simulations. Form \(D_\ell=\ell(\ell+1)\tilde C_\ell/(2\pi)\). Define one scalar statistic \(S=\ln\left[\frac{\sum_{\ell=2}^{8}(2\ell+1)D_\ell}{\sum_{\ell=2}^{8}(2\ell+1)}\right]-\ln\left[\frac{\sum_{\ell=9}^{30}(2\ell+1)D_\ell}{\sum_{\ell=9}^{30}(2\ell+1)}\right]\). This measures whether the very largest angular scales are unusually suppressed or enhanced relative to the rest of the low-\(\ell\) spectrum, with significance obtained by ranking the Planck SMICA value against the 1000 masked LCDM simulations.
test_hypothesis: The Planck SMICA map has an unusually low value of \(S=\ln\langle D_\ell\rangle_{2..8}-\ln\langle D_\ell\rangle_{9..30}\), meaning the very largest angular scales \(\ell=2..8\) are suppressed relative to \(\ell=9..30\) compared with masked LCDM simulations.
justificaton: The statistic is directional: smaller or more negative \(S\) specifically corresponds to suppressed very-large-scale power, and the code identifies the lower tail as the tail of interest for this anomaly.

custom_summary.test_description: Masked Low-l Spectral Tilt Log-Ratio: remove the best-fit monopole and dipole on the unmasked pixels of each masked temperature map, zero-fill the mask, compute the masked pseudo-power spectrum through ell=30, form D_ell=ell(ell+1) Ctilde_ell/(2*pi), and return S=ln(<D_ell>_{2..8,2ell+1})-ln(<D_ell>_{9..30,2ell+1}). Negative S means the very largest angular scales are suppressed relative to ell=9..30.
custom_summary.statistic: S = ln(weighted mean D_ell for 2<=ell<=8) - ln(weighted mean D_ell for 9<=ell<=30), with weights 2ell+1
custom_summary.tail_of_interest: lower tail for unusually suppressed very-large-scale power relative to ell=9..30; upper tail for enhancement
custom_summary.monopole_dipole_treatment: best-fit monopole plus Cartesian dipole removed on unmasked pixels before zero-filling the mask and computing the pseudo-spectrum
custom_summary.mask_treatment: input finite pixels define a binary mask; masked NaN pixels are zero-filled only after monopole/dipole subtraction

plot_spec.title: Masked low-l spectral tilt log-ratio
plot_spec.caption: LCDM simulation distribution of the masked low-l spectral tilt statistic. More negative values indicate stronger suppression of ell=2..8 relative to ell=9..30.

custom_summary.interpretation: Planck is not in either 5% tail for this masked low-l spectral tilt statistic.

test_summary.Description: After masked monopole/dipole removal, pseudo-\(C_\ell\) for \(2 \le \ell \le 30\) was converted to \(D_\ell\) and summarized by \(S=\ln\langle D_\ell\rangle_{2..8}-\ln\langle D_\ell\rangle_{9..30}\); lower \(S\) tests suppression of the very largest scales relative to the rest of low-\(\ell\).
test_summary.Results: Planck SMICA gives \(S=-0.2859\) (low/high ratio \(=0.751\)), versus 1000 LCDM simulations with mean \(-0.1557\), std \(0.1808\), and median \(-0.1451\). It lies at the 22.7th percentile, rank 228 from the low tail; one-tailed lower \(p=0.227\), about \(-0.75\sigma\).
test_summary.Interpretation: Planck is mildly below the simulation mean, but far from the 5% lower tail. This statistic gives no significant evidence that \(\ell=2..8\) is unusually suppressed relative to \(\ell=9..30\).
test_summary.Comparison to literature: Related to the well-known large-angle/low-\(\ell\) power deficit discussed by Planck 2015 XVI (arXiv:1506.07135) and Planck 2018 VII (arXiv:1906.02552), where low power/variance anomalies are reported in some statistics. The present masked log-ratio band test is less significant and does not reproduce an anomalous p-value.
